ddls:
CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the education level of the individual */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the occupation of the individual */,
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the relationship status of the individual */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual */,
  "native_country" TEXT /* Country of origin */,
  "salary_class_id" INTEGER /* Reference to salary classification */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnic background of the individual */,
  "work_experience_years" INTEGER /* Number of years of work experience */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the industry of the individual */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region of the individual */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source of the individual */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_salary_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("salary_class_id") REFERENCES salary_classes ("salary_class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_relationship_id FOREIGN KEY ("relationship_id") REFERENCES relationship_statuses ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., High School, Graduate) */,
  "education_duration_years" INTEGER /* Typical duration of the education level in years */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation (e.g., Nontechnical, Technical) */,
  "occupation_category" TEXT /* Broader category of the occupation (e.g., Healthcare, Technology) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationship_statuses" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship status */,
  "relationship_name" TEXT /* Name of the relationship status (e.g., Not-in-family, Married) */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the relationship status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_classes" (
  "salary_class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary class */,
  "salary_class_range" TEXT /* Description of the salary class range (e.g., <=50K, >50K) */,
  "salary_range_low" TEXT /* Lower bound of the salary range */,
  "salary_range_high" TEXT /* Upper bound of the salary range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Northeast, South) */,
  "region_population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., National Census) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source for reference */,
  "source_reliability_score" TEXT /* Reliability score of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey */,
  "survey_name" TEXT /* Name of the survey (e.g., Labor Market Survey 2023) */,
  "date_conducted" TEXT /* Date when the survey was conducted */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the survey was conducted */,
  "survey_duration_days" INTEGER /* Duration of the survey in days */,
  "survey_sample_size" INTEGER /* Number of participants in the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_surveys_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey to which this response pertains */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual providing the response */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the response was recorded */,
  "response_comments" TEXT /* Additional comments provided by the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration_minutes" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
